bridge_concepts: looping-independent E-P contacts via phase separation
KDM6B H3K27me3 erasure as mechanoenhancer derepression

landmark_paper: Cosgrove et al. 2025 Science — mechanoenhancers genome-wide (ATAC-seq+CRISPRi)

hypotheses.title: The KDM6B→EP300 Sequential Epigenetic Relay Encodes ECM Stiffness at Mechanoenhancers
hypotheses.core_claim: ECM stiffness activates a two-enzyme sequential epigenetic relay at mechanoenhancers: KDM6B (H3K27me3 demethylase, directly controlled by ECM stiffness; Tayler 2026) first removes Polycomb repression (H3K27me3) that maintains mechanoenhancers in a poised/silenced state on soft ECM, then EP300 (directly recruited by YAP1; STRING score 0.692) writes H3K27ac at the newly accessible loci — converting poised mechanoenhancers to fully active ones.
hypotheses.mechanism_summary: Soft ECM: H3K27me3 Polycomb-silenced mechanoenhancers. Stiff ECM: (1) KDM6B upregulated → H3K27me3 demethylation; (2) YAP nuclear → EP300 recruitment → H3K27ac deposition; (3) BRD4 reads H3K27ac → mechanoenhancer fully activated.
hypotheses.primary_prediction: H3K27me3 ChIP-seq in cells on soft ECM (1 kPa) shows enrichment at mechanoenhancers; transfer to stiff ECM (50 kPa) reduces H3K27me3 and increases H3K27ac at these loci within 2-4 hours. KDM6B knockdown on stiff ECM maintains H3K27me3 at mechanoenhancers even when YAP is nuclear.
hypotheses.test_assay: CUT&RUN for H3K27me3 and H3K27ac time course (0,1,2,4,8h after soft→stiff transfer) ± KDM6B siRNA; intersect with Cosgrove 2025 mechanoenhancer catalog.
grounded_claims: KDM6B activity controlled by ECM stiffness: Tayler et al. 2026
KDM6B-EP300 interaction: STRING 0.754
YAP1-EP300 interaction: STRING 0.692
Mechanoenhancers genome-wide: Cosgrove et al. 2025
inferred_claims: Mechanoenhancers in H3K27me3 poised state on soft ECM: not yet shown, consistent with bivalent enhancer biology
counter_evidence: KDM6B studies characterize activity at promoters/gene bodies, not enhancers specifically
Some mechanoenhancers may be in accessible H3K27ac-negative primed state rather than Polycomb-repressed

hypotheses.title: Piezo1→CaMKII→EP300 Rapid Pre-Priming Establishes H3K27ac at Mechanoenhancers Before YAP Nuclear Entry
hypotheses.core_claim: ECM stiffness activates a two-phase mechanoenhancer activation cascade where Piezo1-mediated Ca2+ influx pre-primes mechanoenhancers with H3K27ac within 15 minutes (via CaMKII→EP300; STRING EP300-CAMK2A: 0.908) before YAP nuclear translocation (~30-60 min). This pre-priming creates H3K27ac 'landing pads' that BRD4 pre-occupies, enabling rapid YAP-condensate nucleation when YAP enters the nucleus.
hypotheses.mechanism_summary: Phase 1 (<15 min): ECM → Piezo1 Ca2+ → CaMKII → EP300 activation → H3K27ac at mechanoenhancers. Phase 2 (30-120 min): YAP nuclear → binds BRD4 already at pre-primed H3K27ac → YAP-BRD4-MED1 condensate amplification.
hypotheses.primary_prediction: H3K27ac appears at specific mechanoenhancers within 15 min of ECM stiffness increase; signal is Piezo1-dependent (GsMTx4-blocked) and YAP-independent at 15 min. CaMKII inhibitor (KN-93) phenocopies Piezo1 inhibition for early H3K27ac.
hypotheses.test_assay: CUT&RUN for H3K27ac time course (0,5,15,30,60,120 min) on 50 kPa hydrogels ± GsMTx4 ± KN-93 ± verteporfin; focus on CYR61, CTGF, MYH9 mechanoenhancers.
grounded_claims: EP300-CAMK2A interaction: STRING 0.908 (database + text-mining)
Piezo1 → Rho-ROCK → H3K acetylation: Science Advances 2025
Piezo1 → Ca2+ → CaMKII is canonical signaling pathway
EP300 is canonical H3K27 acetyltransferase at enhancers
inferred_claims: CaMKII phosphorylates EP300 at enhancer loci specifically: plausible but not demonstrated
Piezo1 activation under static ECM stiffness: may be transient (initial cell spreading) rather than sustained
counter_evidence: Piezo1 primarily responds to dynamic compression/membrane tension, not static stiffness
Nuclear CaMKII translocation adds time delay to proposed rapid (<15 min) mechanism
Piezo1 Ca2+ also activates NFAT, calcineurin, eNOS — confounds CaMKII-specific attribution

hypotheses.title: YAP-BRD4 Condensate Size Supralinearly Encodes ECM Stiffness, Creating a Mechanical Switch at Mechanoenhancers
hypotheses.core_claim: YAP nuclear concentration increases ~linearly with ECM stiffness (~4x from 1→50 kPa). BRD4-YAP-MED1 phase-separated condensate assembly at mechanoenhancers is cooperative (Hill coefficient n=2-4), creating supralinear transcriptional amplification — functioning as a mechanical threshold switch at ~8-15 kPa, not a graded rheostat. This explains why fibrotic ECM triggers dramatically different transcriptional programs than healthy ECM despite only a 3-10x stiffness difference.
hypotheses.mechanism_summary: YAP nuclear ∝ stiffness^0.5-1.0 (linear). Condensate volume ∝ [YAP]^n where n=2-4 (cooperative LLPS). Transcriptional output ∝ stiffness^(1.0-3.0). Switch inflection at ~10 kPa critical concentration for condensate nucleation.
hypotheses.primary_prediction: Transcriptional output of mechanoenhancer-controlled genes (CTGF, CYR61 by smFISH) on 5-point stiffness gradient (1, 5, 10, 20, 50 kPa) fits power law model (output ∝ stiffness^n, n>1.5) significantly better than linear model. BRD4 condensate volume (super-resolution) scales supralinearly with nuclear YAP. JQ1 linearizes the relationship.
hypotheses.test_assay: smFISH for CTGF/CYR61 + nuclear YAP quantification across 5-point stiffness gradient; STORM for BRD4 condensate volume at CYR61 locus; ± JQ1 (250 nM) linearization test.
grounded_claims: YAP-BRD4 condensates at super-enhancers: Zanconato et al. 2018 (269 citations)
BRD4-YAP1 interaction: STRING 0.691
EP300-BRD4 interaction: STRING 0.988 (very high)
Phase-separated condensates are cooperative (threshold behavior): Shin et al. 2018 Cell
inferred_claims: Condensate cooperativity n=2-4 for BRD4-YAP: estimated from generic LLPS biophysics, specific value not measured
Threshold stiffness ~8-15 kPa: predicted from YAP concentrations and BRD4 critical concentration estimates
counter_evidence: YAP nuclear concentration may plateau at stiffness >20 kPa — power law may not hold across full range
Whether BRD4 bodies are true LLPS condensates or protein clusters is unresolved
BRD4 condensates are sensitive to ATP levels, temperature, PTMs — non-mechanical variation

hypotheses.title: MRTF-A Preferentially Occupies Mechanoenhancers over Promoters on Stiff ECM, Defining a Non-TEAD Mechanical Enhancer Program
hypotheses.core_claim: MRTF-A (mechanosensitive transcriptional coactivator, nuclear on stiff ECM) binds CaRG-box motifs at mechanoenhancers preferentially over promoters on stiff ECM — driven by nuclear actin concentration. MRTF-A at enhancers directly recruits EP300 (STRING: 0.710, independent of SRF) to deposit H3K27ac, activating a cytoskeletal/contractility mechanoenhancer program spatially and functionally distinct from the YAP/TEAD program.
hypotheses.mechanism_summary: Stiff ECM → F-actin → G-actin depletion → MRTF-A RPEL released → MRTF-A nuclear. MRTF-A binds CaRG-box mechanoenhancers AND promoters. At mechanoenhancers, MRTF-A recruits EP300 directly (0.710) → H3K27ac → CaRG-box mechanoenhancer activation. Parallel to YAP/TEAD program at different cis-elements.
hypotheses.primary_prediction: MRTF-A ChIP-seq on 1 vs 50 kPa hydrogels shows significant enrichment at CaRG-box-containing enhancers (H3K4me1+, >1 kb from TSS, from Cosgrove 2025 catalog) in addition to known CaRG-box promoters. Ratio of MRTF-A peaks at enhancers vs promoters increases on stiff ECM.
hypotheses.test_assay: MRTF-A ChIP-seq (fibroblasts, 1 vs 50 kPa, 24h) → peak annotation (promoter vs enhancer) → intersect with Cosgrove 2025 catalog. MRTF-A + EP300 co-ChIP at CaRG-box mechanoenhancers. MRTF-A siRNA → H3K27ac loss at CaRG-box but not TEAD mechanoenhancers.
grounded_claims: MRTF-A nuclear translocation is F-actin/stiffness-dependent: Miralles et al. 2003 Cell; multiple reviews
EP300-MRTFA interaction: STRING 0.710 (co-expression + experimental)
SRF/CaRG-box motifs enriched at stiff-ECM mechanoenhancers: Cosgrove et al. 2025 motif analysis
SRF-MRTFA interaction: STRING 0.999 (canonical complex)
inferred_claims: MRTF-A binds enhancers at different ratios depending on ECM stiffness: not demonstrated; MRTF-A ChIP-seq in mechanical context not published
counter_evidence: MRTF-A ChIP-seq in non-mechanical contexts primarily shows promoter binding at SRF target genes
CaRG-box motif enrichment (Cosgrove 2025) from motif scanning, not direct MRTF/SRF ChIP
F-actin-driven MRTF activation responds to growth factors and serum in addition to stiffness

hypotheses.title: Phase-Separated YAP Nuclear Condensates Mediate Looping-Independent Multi-Enhancer Hubs, Resolving the 86% Mechanoenhancer Loop-Less Paradox
hypotheses.short_title: YAP Condensates Mediate Looping-Independent E-P Contacts
hypotheses.core_claim: The 86.2% of mechanoenhancer-gene connections that lack annotated chromatin loops (Cosgrove 2025) are mediated by YAP-BRD4 phase-separated nuclear condensates that physically co-localize non-looped mechanoenhancers in 3D nuclear space via surface tension-driven coalescence (Shin et al. 2018). On stiff ECM, YAP condensates pull multiple non-looped mechanoenhancers into a shared nuclear compartment — creating transcriptional co-activation without stable E-P loops detectable by Micro-C.
hypotheses.mechanism_summary: Stiff ECM → YAP nuclear → YAP-BRD4-MED1 condensate nucleation at euchromatic mechanoenhancer loci. Condensate surface tension (~10^-6 N/m → ~10 pN force for 200 nm condensate) transiently pulls neighboring chromatin loci (<500 nm) into proximity without stable cohesin loops. Creates Micro-C 'dark contacts' — dynamic proximity below threshold for loop detection.
hypotheses.primary_prediction: Super-resolution imaging (Oligopaint ORCA) of non-looped mechanoenhancer pairs shows spatial proximity (<300 nm) on stiff ECM absent on soft ECM and dissolved by verteporfin. Verteporfin on stiff ECM dissolves YAP condensates → Micro-C shows loss of non-looped mechanoenhancer contacts without gain of cohesin loops.
hypotheses.test_assay: Oligopaint FISH for 5 non-looped mechanoenhancer pairs (stiff vs soft vs verteporfin-stiff); 3-color ORCA imaging; inter-enhancer distance distributions. YAP STORM super-resolution for condensate-locus overlap. Micro-C before/after verteporfin.
grounded_claims: 86.2% of mechanoenhancer-gene connections lack annotated loops: Cosgrove et al. 2025 (their Micro-C data)
Phase-separated condensates pull genomic loci together via surface tension without looping: Shin et al. 2018 Cell (CasDrop optogenetic system)
YAP-BRD4-MED1 condensates at super-enhancers: Zanconato et al. 2018
Condensates preferentially form in euchromatic (softer) genomic regions: Shin et al. 2018
inferred_claims: YAP condensates specifically co-localize non-looped mechanoenhancers: the novel hypothesis — not yet tested
Condensate surface tension ~10 pN sufficient to pull chromatin loci into proximity: estimated from published condensate parameters
counter_evidence: Looping-independent E-P regulation could also be explained by transcription factories, eRNA-mediated contacts, or TAD proximity effects — phase separation is one of several competing mechanisms
YAP condensates demonstrated in cancer cells on rigid plastic; not established for fibroblasts on physiological-stiffness hydrogels
Verteporfin has multiple targets beyond YAP condensate disruption; off-target effects possible
Micro-C resolution (~200 bp) may be insufficient to detect dynamic transient contacts from condensate surface tension

generation_summary.landmark_anomaly: 86.2% of mechanoenhancer-gene connections lack annotated chromatin loops (Cosgrove 2025)

phase_1_rapid.pathway: Piezo1 → Ca2+ → CaMKII (STRING EP300-CAMK2A: 0.908) → EP300 activation → H3K27ac

phase_2_sustained.pathway: YAP nuclear → BRD4 condensate (STRING BRD4-YAP1: 0.691) + MRTF → EP300 at CaRG (0.710) + KDM6B → H3K27me3 erasure (0.754)
